archetype: Automated Ocean Current Profiling and Tidal Energy Harvesting System for Coastal Renewable Energy Farms
domain: Tidal Energy Harvesting and Coastal Oceanography
description: This system monitors and analyzes ocean currents, tidal patterns, and water levels to optimize the energy harvesting of tidal energy turbines. It is used by coastal renewable energy farms to maximize energy production and reduce maintenance costs. The system must accurately model complex ocean dynamics and integrate with various sensor systems.
rules: MUST comply with the International Electrotechnical Commission (IEC) 61400-22 standard for tidal energy converters
MUST integrate with Acoustic Doppler Current Profiler (ADCP) sensors to measure ocean currents
MUST use the Finite Element Method (FEM) to model tidal flow and energy extraction
MUST adhere to the International Organization for Standardization (ISO) 9001:2015 quality management standard
MUST ensure data security and integrity in accordance with the NERC CIP standards for critical infrastructure
anti_patterns: DO NOT use oversimplified tidal models that neglect nonlinear effects and turbulence
DO NOT rely solely on historical data without incorporating real-time sensor inputs
DO NOT neglect the impact of marine life and seabed topography on energy harvesting
key_technical_challenges: Accurately modeling complex ocean dynamics and turbulence
Integrating multiple sensor systems and data sources
Optimizing energy harvesting while minimizing environmental impact

recommended_stack.language: Python - suitable for scientific computing and data analysis
recommended_stack.framework: Flask - lightweight and flexible for real-time data processing
recommended_stack.database_engine: PostgreSQL - robust and scalable for large datasets
recommended_stack.cache: Redis - efficient for caching sensor data and intermediate results
recommended_stack.message_broker: Apache Kafka - suitable for handling high-volume sensor data
